title: pellaea rotundifolia
intro: Pellaea rotundifolia, also known as the round-leaved fern or native fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as soothing coughs, treating bronchitis, and acting as a natural remedy for skin irritations and wounds. Pellaea rotundifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and promote recovery.
